communications.subject: Illinois Freedom of Information Act. Request: Chicago Mayor Email Record Request 1/1/2025-6/1/2026
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Illinois Freedom of Information Act., I hereby request the following records:

I am seeking records under the Illinois Freedom of Information Act related to Automatic License Plate readers or ALPRs. For context, license plate readers technology is of large public importance and has been the subject of numerous news reports and at the center citizen action against them. For example, the use of ALPR has been getting kicked out of and investigated in the Chicago-suburbs for over a year (Oak park, Evanston and Homer Glen putting it on hold) because of privacy concerns related to police departments spying on residents and the technology used by ICE and DHS to deport citizens in violation of the Illinois TRUST act.  Major cities nationwide like Seattle & Denver have also ended their use of ALPRs for these same reasons.
The Illinois Secretary of State performed an audit which found that neighborhoods as close as Forest Park allowed DHS and ICE to access their license plate reader data. In order for Chicagoans to fully understand the City's relationship to Flock and the use of Automated License Plate Readers generally, we must understand how our City government is discussing the technology, who they are talking to, and how the City is using the technology.
Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I am requesting the following records:
All email messages from the Mayor's Office of Public Safety & Deputy Mayor Garien Gatewood (garien.gatewood@cityofchicago.com) between the dates 1/1/2025 and 6/1/2026 including the words "ICE" & "DHS" or "CBP" or "Customs and border protection" or "Immigration and Customs Enforcement". Please include any variations that are not case sensitive.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 5 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Candice Garcia
